text: Overtraining syndrome is triggered by multiple factors beyond excessive training volume, with chronic energy deprivation being a primary driver.
Approximately 72.7% of markers affected in overtraining syndrome represent a loss of exercise-induced conditioning effects ('paradoxical deconditioning'), rather than novel pathological processes.
Overtraining syndrome leads to gradual and marked loss of physical conditioning through reversal of normal training adaptations, not just fatigue or temporary performance decrements.
Inadequate energy availability (chronic energy deprivation) combined with excessive training is a more accurate model for overtraining syndrome than excessive training alone.
Only 15.9% of markers altered in overtraining syndrome represent changes exclusive to the syndrome, while the majority reflect reversal of healthy training adaptations.

Overtraining syndrome involves widespread hormonal and metabolic disruption, with 65.7% of 67 evaluated parameters showing significant differences between overtrained athletes and healthy athletes or controls.
